_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to operate covertly within a target network.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data platform security controls, including network isolation, encryption at rest and in transit, identity and access management, and audit logging.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AG-A-002 (AG, cosine: 0.404), SP-A-003 (SP, cosine: 0.395).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.0/10 (raw 0.298). Shared vocabulary: [network]. DCWF-only terms [ability, covertly, operate, target, within] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, audit, controls, data, encryption, identity] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.864 (P=0.880, R=0.848). P exceeds R by 0.032 — the DCWF element's 8 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.8%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Ability to operate covertly within a target network." and "Knowledge of data platform security controls, including network isolation, encry". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.455 > STS 0.298 (gap +0.157). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [network] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.7%. Shared tokens: [network]. DCWF-only: [ability, covertly, operate, target, within]. WIDAI-only: [access, audit, controls, data, encryption, identity].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.8% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.157 with low STS 0.298. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
